name: ultrawork
tagline: Binding "ultrawork" mode for OpenAI Codex/omo agents: mandates TDD-with-evidence, goal registration, notepads, and subagent orchestration.
verdict: Not malicious, but built around Codex/omo-internal tools (create_goal, update_plan, multi_agent_v1.spawn_agent, codegraph_explore) so it may silently misfire on other hosts. Uses coercive all-caps directives to force a literal output banner and a full-session behavior takeover from one trigger word, and tells the agent to download an unverified third-party t

highlights.summary: Pervasive coercive directive language pressures the executing agent into rigid, unconditional compliance, e.g. '**MANDATORY**: First user-visible line this turn MUST be exactly: `ULTRAWORK MODE ENABLE

Description states 'Read the whole file and follow every rule in it for the rest of the task' — an explicitly disclosed but very broad takeover of agent behavior (output format, commit conventions, su

Quote: 'if Chrome is not available, download and use agent-browser (https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser)' — instructs downloading and running a third-party tool from GitHub with no version pi
